questions.q: What is the primary objective of rural development?
questions.answer: To improve the quality of life of rural people

questions.q: Which of the following is a major characteristic of rural development?
questions.answer: Involves large-scale migration to urban areas

questions.q: Explain the concept of rural poverty in India.
questions.answer: Rural poverty refers to the condition where people living in rural areas are unable to meet their basic needs such as food, shelter, clothing, and healthcare. It is a complex issue in India due to various factors such as low agricultural productivity, lack of employment opportunities, and inadequate access to healthcare and education.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: Rural development involves ______________ of rural infrastructure, such as roads, bridges, and irrigation systems.
What is the role of cooperatives in rural development?
questions.answer: Cooperatives play a vital role in rural development by providing financial services, marketing facilities, and technical assistance to rural people.

questions.q: Which of the following is a major challenge facing rural development in India?
questions.answer: Lack of access to electricity and sanitation

questions.q: Explain the concept of rural-urban migration in the context of rural development.
questions.answer: Rural-urban migration refers to the movement of people from rural areas to urban areas in search of employment, better living conditions, and access to amenities such as healthcare and education. This migration can have both positive and negative impacts on rural development, depending on the context and circumstances.

questions.q: True or False: Rural development is limited to the improvement of agricultural productivity.
What is the significance of rural entrepreneurship in rural development?
questions.answer: Rural entrepreneurship is crucial for rural development as it creates employment opportunities, promotes innovation, and stimulates economic growth in rural areas.

questions.q: Explain the concept of rural poverty alleviation programs in India.
questions.answer: Rural poverty alleviation programs refer to initiatives and schemes implemented by the government to reduce poverty and improve the living standards of rural people. These programs include poverty reduction programs, employment guarantee schemes, and social security programs, among others.
